our_open_deliverables.text: Share the 23 June SRM demo recording and demo artifacts with SAMI attendees who requested follow-up materials.
Confirm and document whether Zycus supports historical price view (historical price book / PO-level price history) without relying on AI, and provide implementation approach and examples.
Provide details on recent S/4HANA integrations (including any Saudi implementations) and confirm iSaaS adapter capabilities and required API design inputs for SAMI's S/4 landscape.

Coordinate with SAMI stakeholders to collect any additional commercial or clarification requests and route them to the Zycus product/technical team for written replies before mid-July decision window.

buyer_dependent_items.text: Provide any remaining evaluation clarifications or commercial questions by email before the mid-July decision window.

competitive_position.summary: SAMI is running a competitive three-vendor shortlist (confirmed 2 March 2026). GEP Worldwide is the strongest current threat: heavy services support (critical for defense complexity and EY partner alignment) and likely proven defense ERP integration capability (SAMI's repeated S/4HANA consignment/MRP questions on 23 Jun suggest benchmarking against a vendor that has delivered this). SAP Ariba is in the mix but SAMI is skeptical (CRM: 'they ask for detail on why not Ariba, clearly they are unliked', likely due to cost/complexity or IBM relationship). Coupa is flagged for strong A&D customers but no call evidence shows Coupa as front-runner. iValua is noted for heavy customisation/localisation fit but not elevated above GEP. Zycus's wedge is the ST Engineering defense reference (13 Jan call: synchronized ERP/procurement go-live, compliance customizations) and AI/Merlin (Next_Step 26 Feb: 'investigating how we can drive a strong AI message'), BUT CRM blockers (15 May) warn 'ZERO AI functionality will be accepted', and 23 Jun call shows SAMI asking for non-AI historical price view, suggesting AI is optional or a concern. Zycus must position Merlin as optional, prove defense-grade S/4HANA integration in writing to match GEP, and lean on ST Engineering to win.

competitors.name: GEP Worldwide
competitors.quote: Suspect the key players are: GEP (Heavy services support)
competitors.how_we_win: Prove defense-grade S/4HANA consignment/MRP integration capability in writing (match GEP's services depth); lead with ST Engineering defense reference (synchronized ERP/procurement go-live, compliance guarantees); position Merlin AI as optional accelerator (not mandatory, addressing SAMI's AI caution per 23 Jun call); deliver flawless technical/commercial responses by mid-July.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.how_we_win: Highlight Zycus's ST Engineering defense reference and defense-specific compliance capabilities vs Coupa's general A&D positioning; emphasize AI/Merlin differentiation if SAMI's AI caution softens.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.quote: Ariba (relationship with SAP/IBM — but they ask for detail on why not Ariba, clearly they are unliked)
competitors.how_we_win: Highlight Zycus's defense-fit and ST Engineering reference vs Ariba's generic S/4HANA offering; emphasize lower cost and faster deployment vs Ariba/IBM.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.how_we_win: Position Zycus's single-codebase configurability (not custom code) as faster and lower-risk than iValua's heavy customisation; lead with ST Engineering reference and one-platform story.

champion_strength.source: CRM MEDDPICC 2.0 Champion (15 May 2026); Avoma 13 Jan 2026, 23 Jun 2026
champion_strength.summary: No champion identified. Franck Devienne (Senior Procurement Advisor, CRM Economic Buyer) is running an arms-length competitive RFP and has shown no advocacy (CRM 15 May: 'Carl has had positive conversations with Franck, but he doesn't fit the champion role'). The other named SAMI stakeholders (Yousef Alarabi, Ahmed Almania, AhmedKamal) have been present on calls but have asked only functional/technical questions; none has emerged as a coach or advocate. Without a champion, Zycus has no visibility into undocumented decision factors (GEP's pitch, internal politics, the unmapped CFO's preferences) and cannot preempt competitor moves. Developing a champion is the highest-priority action before the 20 July decision.

sources: CRM MEDDPICC 2.0 Economic buyer: 'Franck Devienne Senior Procurement Advisor' (15 May 2026)
Avoma 23 Jun 2026 (speaker_id 11): 'approval of the negotiated price requires evidence to support it' (higher authority required)
Contact roles: Franck Devienne (Senior Procurement Advisor), Yousef Alarabi (Sr. Procurement Specialist), Ahmed Almania (Procurement Specialist) — no CFO/CEO/C-level
Avoma 13 Jan 2026, 23 Jun 2026: Franck present and leading; no senior exec joined
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Franck Devienne (Senior Procurement Advisor, fdevienne@sami.com.sa) is the CRM-identified Economic Buyer (MEDDPICC 2.0, 15 May 2026) and has been the primary SAMI contact throughout the RFP cycle. Franck led the 13 January 2026 intro call, the 23 June 2026 SRM demo, and all email correspondence (Next_Step__c references 'ongoing clarifications with advisor Franck' on 6 Jun, and 'Franck reached out' on 21 May). CRM states 'Centrally held, but Franck and team will determine the award decision.' However, on the 23 June 2026 call a SAMI buyer (speaker_id 11) stated 'approval of the negotiated price requires evidence to support it, which adds an additional burden on the buyer' (Avoma objection note), confirming that Franck cannot approve the $2.2M spend alone and a higher authority (CFO, CEO, or C-level sponsor) must sign off. No CFO, CEO, or C-level executive has appeared in any call, contact role, or task. Franck is the operational Economic Buyer (controls the evaluation and vendor selection), but the FINANCIAL Economic Buyer (budget signatory) is unmapped. On a $2.2M defense procurement deal at Shortlisted stage (mid-July decision), the absence of a mapped C-level sponsor is a real commercial risk: the unmapped executive could introduce new commercial conditions, strategic vendor preferences, or reject the pricing post-selection. Zycus must map and engage the CFO or equivalent before final commercials.

recommended_moves: Deliver written responses to defense-specific S/4HANA integration questions by 10 July.
Identify and engage the CFO or C-level budget authority above Franck Devienne.
Develop a champion: identify which SAMI stakeholder sees the most value in Zycus and build their advocacy.
